name: Pennsylvania
tooltip: Pennsylvania — Cat 3: 42 Pa.C.S. § 8316. 30-year post-mortem. No AI-specific overlay yet.
rightOfPublicity: Yes — 42 Pa.C.S. § 8316. Covers commercial value of name or likeness for living and deceased persons.
aiCoverage: No general AI digital replica statute. 42 Pa.C.S. § 8316.1 is a separate intimate-image statute. *Hepp v. Facebook*, 14 F.4th 204 (3d Cir. 2021) is the controlling Third Circuit case on the § 230 IP-exception interaction with state right-of-publicity claims — favorable to plaintiffs.
remedy: Civil suit under § 8316 for actual damages plus profits and attorney's fees. The Third Circuit's *Hepp* posture means platforms don't get automatic § 230 immunity for state ROP claims, which is plaintiff-friendly.
whatsInteresting: Pennsylvania doesn't have an AI-specific likeness statute yet, but it has something almost as useful: the Third Circuit's *Hepp v. Facebook* ruling, which says state right-of-publicity claims survive § 230 immunity because of the IP exception. This is huge if you're suing a platform for hosting unauthorized AI content of you — most circuits dismiss these on § 230, the Third Circuit doesn't. For Pennsylvania-based performers, that procedural advantage matters more than the absence of an AI-specific statute.
